generatedSentence: The reclusive architect, a true genius, meticulously studied the ancient herbarium, his daily routines fueled by the subtle rhythm of old lathes in his hidden facilities. He often tinkered with geothermal energy schematics, convinced a new plan could free the people from their distant tyranny. His work, a narrative of quiet defiance, was a dogma he passionately pursued, despite the noise of the city's powerplay always intruding. He felt a peculiar humor in his solitary existence, a grumpy but unbroken spirit, especially after an unlucky turn of events had left him with nothing but an old toothbrush and a faint scent of marjoram from his garden. One early daybreak, a group of suspicious kidnappers in a dark airship attempted to hijack his work, their movements as precise as a ninja. Their goal was to gain input on his energy calculations, believing his finite knowledge could power their rocket. He watched with bewilderment as they displayed their mammalian agility, scaling the old brick wall, accompanied by their trained beagles. This sudden horror was literally happening; his newfound options were dwindling fast. The lawmaker behind this arbitration sought total control, turning his secluded life into a desperate powerplay where his own efficacy was now in question, making him truly needy for an escape. Daringly, he initiated his escape plan, sliding through a hidden tunnel while emitting a low-frequency melody to distract the jesters who guarded the warehousing unit. He felt the rock beneath his feet, his paddle forgotten as he ran. His potbelly notwithstanding, he moved with surprising agility, his mind a whirlwind of retrospective moments. The kilowatts of stored gasoline were a tempting target for his pursuers. He knew the price of failure; being undone was not an option. Instead, he activated a prototype jetpack, a miracle of niobium alloys, feeling the sudden swing of acceleration as he left the locomotive of his problems behind. He was unchained, flying bravely towards a precarious freedom, away from the grim divisions of the city. He landed softly in a forgotten nursery, feeling the nature all around him, a welcome respite from the viral reach of the city's tyranny. Maybe it was a miracle, but he had evaded the nonjury court that would surely have condemned him, the gentlemen in charge now facing a vacancy in their plans. He realized his daring escape would be his narrative to the publisher, a tale for people to rejoice. He still had a deep owe to the anonymous actor who had helped him, the oracles of the resistance. His future, like a perfectly braised meal, was now his to savor, though the memory of vancomycin they tried to force on him gave him lockjaw. He wouldn't be a nobody again. He was free, proving that even the most farfetched dreams could be realized.
panelSentences: The reclusive architect, a true genius, meticulously studied the ancient herbarium, his daily routines fueled by the subtle rhythm of old lathes in his hidden facilities. He often tinkered with geothermal energy schematics, convinced a new plan could free the people from their distant tyranny. His work, a narrative of quiet defiance, was a dogma he passionately pursued, despite the noise of the city's powerplay always intruding. He felt a peculiar humor in his solitary existence, a grumpy but unbroken spirit, especially after an unlucky turn of events had left him with nothing but an old toothbrush and a faint scent of marjoram from his garden.

One early daybreak, a group of suspicious kidnappers in a dark airship attempted to hijack his work, their movements as precise as a ninja. Their goal was to gain input on his energy calculations, believing his finite knowledge could power their rocket. He watched with bewilderment as they displayed their mammalian agility, scaling the old brick wall, accompanied by their trained beagles. This sudden horror was literally happening; his newfound options were dwindling fast. The lawmaker behind this arbitration sought total control, turning his secluded life into a desperate powerplay where his own efficacy was now in question, making him truly needy for an escape.

Daringly, he initiated his escape plan, sliding through a hidden tunnel while emitting a low-frequency melody to distract the jesters who guarded the warehousing unit. He felt the rock beneath his feet, his paddle forgotten as he ran. His potbelly notwithstanding, he moved with surprising agility, his mind a whirlwind of retrospective moments. The kilowatts of stored gasoline were a tempting target for his pursuers. He knew the price of failure; being undone was not an option. Instead, he activated a prototype jetpack, a miracle of niobium alloys, feeling the sudden swing of acceleration as he left the locomotive of his problems behind. He was unchained, flying bravely towards a precarious freedom, away from the grim divisions of the city.

He landed softly in a forgotten nursery, feeling the nature all around him, a welcome respite from the viral reach of the city's tyranny. Maybe it was a miracle, but he had evaded the nonjury court that would surely have condemned him, the gentlemen in charge now facing a vacancy in their plans. He realized his daring escape would be his narrative to the publisher, a tale for people to rejoice. He still had a deep owe to the anonymous actor who had helped him, the oracles of the resistance. His future, like a perfectly braised meal, was now his to savor, though the memory of vancomycin they tried to force on him gave him lockjaw. He wouldn't be a nobody again. He was free, proving that even the most farfetched dreams could be realized.
rawPanelSentences: The reclusive **architect**, a true **genius**, meticulously **studied** the ancient **herbarium**, his **daily routines** fueled by the subtle **rhythm** of old **lathes** in his hidden **facilities**. He often **tinker**ed with **geothermal** energy schematics, convinced a new **plan** could free the **people** from their distant **tyranny**. His work, a **narrative** of quiet defiance, was a **dogma** he passionately pursued, despite the **noise** of the city's **powerplay** always intruding. He felt a peculiar **humor** in his solitary existence, a **grumpy** but **unbroken** spirit, especially after an **unlucky** turn of events had left him with nothing but an old **toothbrush** and a faint scent of **marjoram** from his garden.

One **early** **daybreak**, a group of **suspicious** **kidnappers** in a dark **airship** attempted to **hijack** his work, their movements as precise as a **ninja**. Their goal was to gain **input** on his energy calculations, believing his **finite** knowledge could power their **rocket**. He watched with **bewilderment** as they displayed their **mammalian** agility, scaling the old **brick** wall, accompanied by their trained **beagles**. This sudden **horror** was **literally** happening; his **newfound** **options** were dwindling **fast**. The **lawmaker** behind this **arbitration** sought **total** control, turning his secluded life into a desperate **powerplay** where his own **efficacy** was now in question, making him truly **needy** for an escape.

Daringly, he initiated his escape **plan**, sliding through a hidden **tunnel** while emitting a low-frequency **melody** to distract the **jesters** who guarded the **warehousing** unit. He felt the **rock** beneath his feet, his **paddle** forgotten as he ran. His **potbelly** notwithstanding, he moved with surprising agility, his mind a whirlwind of **retrospective** moments. The **kilowatts** of stored **gasoline** were a tempting target for his pursuers. He knew the **price** of failure; being **undone** was not an **option**. **Instead**, he activated a prototype **jetpack**, a **miracle** of **niobium** alloys, feeling the sudden **swing** of acceleration as he left the **locomotive** of his problems behind. He was **unchained**, flying **bravely** towards a precarious freedom, away from the grim **divisions** of the city.

He landed softly in a forgotten **nursery**, feeling the **nature** all around him, a welcome respite from the **viral** reach of the city's **tyranny**. **Maybe** it was a **miracle**, but he had evaded the **nonjury** court that would surely have condemned him, the **gentlemen** in charge now facing a **vacancy** in their plans. He realized his daring escape would be his **narrative** to the **publisher**, a tale for **people** to **rejoice**. He still had a deep **owe** to the anonymous actor who had helped him, the **oracles** of the resistance. His future, like a perfectly **braised** meal, was now his to savor, though the memory of **vancomycin** they tried to force on him gave him **lockjaw**. He wouldn't be a **nobody** **again**. He was free, proving that even the most **farfetched** dreams could be realized.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, thick white gutters, clean black borders. A lone male protagonist, middle-aged, with a slightly disheveled grey beard and a worn leather architect's apron. TOP-LEFT PANEL: A wide shot of the protagonist, hunched over a glowing holographic blueprint, surrounded by antique brass scientific instruments and botanical specimens, a subtle green aura around his hands, viewed from a high angle. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: A dynamic close-up of shadowy figures in futuristic armor rappelling down a grimy brick wall, a pack of robotic beagles barking below, viewed from a low angle. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: A dramatic medium shot of the protagonist mid-air, a makeshift jetpack strapped to his back, smoke billowing, one hand reaching towards a glowing tunnel entrance, viewed from a side angle. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: A serene wide shot of the protagonist sitting in a lush, overgrown botanical garden, a small, blooming plant in his hand, sunlight filtering through the canopy, viewed from a slightly elevated angle, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting.

definitions.definition: A herb of the mint family, Origanum majorana, having aromatic leaves.
The leaves of this plant used in flavouring food.
The cost required to gain possession of something.
The cost of an action or deed.
definitions.example: I paid a high price for my folly.

definitions.definition: Value; estimation; excellence; worth.
To determine the monetary value of (an item); to put a price on.
To pay the price of; to make reparation for.
To set a price on; to value; to prize.
To ask the price of.
definitions.example: to price eggs

definitions.definition: Used as plural of person; a body of human beings considered generally or collectively; a group of two or more persons.
definitions.example: There were so many people at the restaurant last night.

definitions.definition: Persons forming or belonging to a particular group, such as a nation, class, ethnic group, country, family, etc.
A group of persons regarded as being employees, followers, companions or subjects of a ruler.
One's colleagues or employees.
A person's ancestors, relatives or family.
definitions.example: My people lived through the Black Plague and the Thirty Years War.

definitions.definition: The mass of a community as distinguished from a special class (elite); the commonalty; the populace; the vulgar; the common crowd; the citizens.
To stock with people or inhabitants; to fill as with people; to populate.
To become populous or populated.
To inhabit; to occupy; to populate.
To interact with people; to socialize.
An individual; usually a human being.
definitions.example: Each person is unique, both mentally and physically.

definitions.definition: The physical body of a being seen as distinct from the mind, character, etc.
Any individual or formal organization with standing before the courts.
definitions.example: At common law a corporation or a trust is legally a person.

definitions.definition: The human genitalia; specifically, the penis.
(grammar) A linguistic category used to distinguish between the speaker of an utterance and those to whom or about whom he or she is speaking. See grammatical person.
A shoot or bud of a plant; a polyp or zooid of the compound Hydrozoa, Anthozoa, etc.; also, an individual, in the narrowest sense, among the higher animals.
A two-handed, single-bladed oar used to propel a canoe or a small boat.
A double-bladed oar used for kayaking.
Time spent on paddling.
definitions.example: We had a nice paddle this morning.

definitions.definition: A slat of a paddleboat's wheel.
A paddlewheel.
A blade of a waterwheel.
A game controller with a round wheel used to control player movement along one axis of the video screen.
A meandering walk or dabble through shallow water, especially at the seaside.
A kitchen utensil shaped like a paddle and used for mixing, beating etc.
A bat-shaped spanking implement
definitions.example: The paddle practically ousted the British cane for spankings in the independent US.

definitions.definition: A ping-pong bat.
A flat limb of an aquatic animal, adapted for swimming.
definitions.example: A sea turtle's paddles make it swim almost as fast as land tortoises are slow.

definitions.definition: In a sluice, a panel that controls the flow of water.
A group of inerts
A handheld defibrillation/cardioversion electrode
Hand
Padel (sport)
To propel something through water with a paddle, oar, hands, etc.
To row a boat with less than one's full capacity.
To spank with a paddle.
To pat or stroke amorously or gently.
To tread upon; to trample.
Arousing suspicion.
definitions.example: His suspicious behaviour brought him to the attention of the police.

definitions.definition: Distrustful or tending to suspect.
definitions.example: I have a suspicious attitude to get-rich-quick schemes.

definitions.definition: Expressing suspicion
definitions.example: She gave me a suspicious look.

definitions.definition: A collection of dried plants or parts of plants.
A building or institution where such a collection is kept.
To be very happy, be delighted, exult; to feel joy.
To have (someone) as a lover or spouse; to enjoy sexually.
To make happy, exhilarate.
To enjoy.
To remove chains from; to free; to liberate.
Free from chains or fetters; unencumbered.
The systematic recitation of an event or series of events.
That which is narrated.
A representation of an event or story.
(creative writing) A manner of conveying a story, fictional or otherwise, in a body of work.
definitions.example: The plot is full of holes, but the narrative is extremely compelling.

definitions.definition: Telling a story.
Overly talkative; garrulous.
Of or relating to narration.
definitions.example: the narrative thrust of a film

definitions.definition: An adult male human.
definitions.example: The show is especially popular with middle-aged men.

definitions.definition: (collective) All human males collectively: mankind.
A human, a person of either gender, usually an adult. (See usage notes.)
definitions.example: every man for himself

definitions.definition: (collective) All humans collectively: mankind, humankind, humanity. (Sometimes capitalized as Man.)
A member of the genus Homo, especially of the species Homo sapiens.
An male person, usually an adult; a (generally adult male) sentient being, whether human, supernatural, elf, alien, etc.
An adult male who has, to an eminent degree, qualities considered masculine, such as strength, integrity, and devotion to family; a mensch.
Manliness; the quality or state of being manly.
A husband.
A lover; a boyfriend.
A male enthusiast or devotee; a male who is very fond of or devoted to a specified kind of thing. (Used as the last element of a compound.)
definitions.example: Some people prefer apple pie, but me, I’m a cherry pie man.

definitions.definition: A person, usually male, who has duties or skills associated with a specified thing. (Used as the last element of a compound.)
definitions.example: I wanted to be a guitar man on a road tour, but instead I’m a flag man on a road crew.

definitions.definition: A person, usually male, who can fulfill one's requirements with regard to a specified matter.
A male who belongs to a particular group: an employee, a student or alumnus, a representative, etc.
An adult male servant.
A vassal; a subject.
definitions.example: Like master, like man.

definitions.definition: A piece or token used in board games such as chess.
Used to refer to oneself or one's group: I, we; construed in the third person.
A term of familiar address often implying on the part of the speaker some degree of authority, impatience, or haste.
definitions.example: Come on, man, we've got no time to lose!

definitions.definition: A friendly term of address usually reserved for other adult males.
definitions.example: Hey, man, how's it goin'?

definitions.definition: A player on whom another is playing, with the intent of limiting their attacking impact.
A person trained in ninjutsu, especially one used for espionage, assassination, and other tasks requiring stealth during Japan's shogunate period.
A person considered similarly skillful to the historical ninja, especially in covert or stealthy operation.
A person considered to look like the historical ninja in some way, including (Mongolia) amateur private miners.
To act or move like a ninja, particularly with regard to a combination of speed, power, and stealth.
To claim an item through abuse of game mechanics.
definitions.example: That damn warrior ninja'd an epic-quality wand even though he can't even use it!

definitions.definition: Of or related to ninjas in their various senses.
To appropriate something (before someone else does).
To displace something, or take precedence over something.
To secure (land, etc.) by the right of preemption.
To make a preemptive bid at bridge.
Someone who is not important or well-known.
definitions.example: Whether you're a nobody or a player, everyone in Tinseltown wants to be King of Hollywood! - Conquering Hollywood

definitions.definition: Not any person; the logical negation of somebody.
definitions.example: I asked several people, but nobody knew how.

definitions.definition: The act or process of arbitrating.
A process through which two or more parties use an arbitrator or arbiter in order to resolve a dispute.

In general, a form of justice where both parties designate a person whose ruling they will accept formally. More specifically in Market Anarchist (market anarchy) theory, arbitration designates the process by which two agencies pre-negotiate a set of common rules in anticipation of cases where a customer from each agency is involved in a dispute.
An underground or underwater passage.
A passage through or under some obstacle.
A hole in the ground made by an animal, a burrow.
A wrapper for a protocol that cannot otherwise be used because it is unsupported, blocked, or insecure.
A vessel with a broad mouth at one end, a pipe or tube at the other, for conveying liquor, fluids, etc., into casks, bottles, or other vessels; a funnel.
The opening of a chimney for the passage of smoke; a flue.
A level passage driven across the measures, or at right angles to veins which it is desired to reach; distinguished from the drift, or gangway, which is led along the vein when reached by the tunnel.
To make a tunnel through or under something; to burrow.
To dig a tunnel.
To transmit something through a tunnel (wrapper for insecure or unsupported protocol).
To insert a catheter into a vein to allow long-term use.
To undergo the quantum-mechanical phenomenon where a particle penetrates through a barrier that it classically cannot surmount.
A person who kidnaps someone.
To show conspicuously; to exhibit; to demonstrate; to manifest.
To make a display; to act as one making a show or demonstration.
To extend the front of (a column), bringing it into line.
To make conspicuous by using large or prominent type.
To discover; to descry.
To spread out, to unfurl.
Spread out; unfurled.
Spread open to view; shown off.
With wings unfurled.
Set with lines of prominent type interspersed, to catch the eye.
A hardened rectangular block of mud, clay etc., used for building.
definitions.example: This wall is made of bricks.

definitions.definition: Such hardened mud, clay, etc. considered collectively, as a building material.
definitions.example: This house is made of brick.

definitions.definition: Something shaped like a brick.
definitions.example: a plastic explosive brick

definitions.definition: A helpful and reliable person.
definitions.example: Thanks for helping me wash the car. You're a brick.

definitions.definition: A shot which misses, particularly one which bounces directly out of the basket because of a too-flat trajectory, as if the ball were a heavier object.
definitions.example: We can't win if we keep throwing up bricks from three-point land.

definitions.definition: A power brick; an external power supply consisting of a small box with an integral male power plug and an attached electric cord terminating in another power plug.
An electronic device, especially a heavy box-shaped one, that has become non-functional or obsolete.
A carton of 500 rimfire cartridges, which forms the approximate size and shape of a brick.
A community card (usually the turn or the river) which does not improve a player's hand.

definitions.definition: The colour brick red.
One kilo of cocaine.
To build with bricks.
To make into bricks.
To hit someone or something with a brick.
To make an electronic device nonfunctional and usually beyond repair, essentially making it no more useful than a brick.
definitions.example: My VCR was bricked during the lightning storm.

definitions.definition: (of weather) Extremely cold.
The state of being bewildered.
A confusing or perplexing situation.
The act or process of putting in; infusion.
That which is put in, as in an amount.
Contribution of work or information, as an opinion or advice.
Something fed into a process with the intention of it shaping or affecting the output of that process.
To put in; put on.
To enter data.
definitions.example: The user inputs his date of birth and the computer displays his age.

definitions.definition: To accept data that is entered.
Any mammal.
Of, or pertaining to, mammals
Like a mammal
A drawing showing technical details of a building, machine, etc., with unwanted details omitted, and often using symbols rather than detailed drawing to represent doors, valves, etc.
definitions.example: The plans for many important buildings were once publicly available.

definitions.definition: A set of intended actions, usually mutually related, through which one expects to achieve a goal.
definitions.example: He didn't really have a plan; he had a goal and a habit of control.

definitions.definition: A two-dimensional drawing of a building as seen from above with obscuring or irrelevant details such as roof removed, or of a floor of a building, revealing the internal layout; as distinct from the elevation.
definitions.example: Seen in plan, the building had numerous passageways not apparent to visitors.

definitions.definition: A method; a way of procedure; a custom.
A subscription to a service.
definitions.example: a phone plan

definitions.definition: To design (a building, machine, etc.).
definitions.example: The architect planned the building for the client.

definitions.definition: To create a plan for.
definitions.example: They jointly planned the project in phases, with good detail for the first month.

definitions.definition: To intend.
definitions.example: He planned to go, but work intervened.

definitions.definition: To make a plan.
definitions.example: They planned for the worst, bringing lots of emergency supplies.

definitions.definition: One who publishes, especially books.
Tactics used to magnify power or influence.
A strategy or maneuver with a similar intention.
A situation where a team has a numerical advantage on the ice due to one or more penalties given to the opposing team.
A similar situation in other ball games such as lacrosse, indoor soccer, rugby and cricket.
A play used to move the stones to the side of the sheet.
Pertaining to heat energy extracted from reservoirs in the earth's interior
The variation of strong and weak elements (such as duration, accent) of sounds, notably in speech or music, over time; a beat or meter.
definitions.example: Dance to the rhythm of the music.

definitions.definition: A specifically defined pattern of such variation.
definitions.example: Most dances have a rhythm as distinctive as the Iambic verse in poetry

definitions.definition: A flow, repetition or regularity.
definitions.example: Once you get the rhythm of it, the job will become easy.

definitions.definition: The tempo or speed of a beat, song or repetitive event.
definitions.example: We walked with a quick, even rhythm.

definitions.definition: The musical instruments which provide rhythm (mainly; not or less melody) in a musical ensemble.
definitions.example: The Baroque term basso continuo is virtually equivalent to rhythm

definitions.definition: A regular quantitative change in a variable (notably natural) process.
definitions.example: The rhythm of the seasons dominates agriculture as well as wildlife

definitions.definition: Controlled repetition of a phrase, incident or other element as a stylistic figure in literature and other narrative arts; the effect it creates.
definitions.example: The running gag is a popular rhythm in motion pictures and theater comedy

definitions.definition: Someone who engages in rubbernecking, or turning and staring.
A tourist.
Someone or something with a flexible neck.
To watch by craning the neck (as though it were made of rubber), especially if the observer and observed are in motion relative to each other.
definitions.example: The driver was so busy rubbernecking, trying to get a good view of the accident, that he was almost part of another accident.

definitions.definition: A formation of minerals, specifically:
A large hill or island having no vegetation.
definitions.example: Pearl Rock near Cape Cod is so named because the morning sun makes it gleam like a pearl.

definitions.definition: Something that is strong, stable, and dependable; a person who provides security or support to another.
A lump or cube of ice.
definitions.example: I'll have a whisky on the rocks, please.

definitions.definition: A type of confectionery made from sugar in the shape of a stick, traditionally having some text running through its length.
definitions.example: While we're in Brighton, let's get a stick of rock!

definitions.definition: A crystallized lump of crack cocaine.
An unintelligent person, especially one who repeats mistakes.
An Afrikaner.
An extremely conservative player who is willing to play only the very strongest hands.
Any of several fish:
A basketball.
definitions.example: Yo homie, pass the rock!

definitions.definition: (rock paper scissors) A closed hand (a handshape resembling a rock), that beats scissors and loses to paper. It beats lizard and loses to Spock in rock-paper-scissors-lizard-Spock.
A cricket ball, especially a new one that has not been softened by use
A crystal used to control the radio frequency.
Not done.
Various sounds, usually unwanted or unpleasant.

definitions.definition: Sound or signal generated by random fluctuations.
Unwanted part of a signal.
(by extension) Unwanted fuss or bustle; useless activity.
The measured level of variation in gene expression among cells, regardless of source, within a supposedly identical population.
Rumour or complaint.
definitions.example: The problems with the new computer system are causing a lot of noise at Head Office.

definitions.definition: Music, in general; a concert; also, a company of musicians; a band.
A genre of rock music that uses static and other non-musical sounds, also influenced by art rock.
To make a noise; to sound.
To spread news of; to spread as rumor or gossip.
A professional who designs buildings or other structures, or who prepares plans and superintends construction.
definitions.example: Plato made the causes of things to be matter, ideas, and an efficient architect.

definitions.definition: A person who plans, devises or contrives the achievement of a desired result.
definitions.example: Peisistratus was the first architect of the Iliad and the Odyssey.

definitions.definition: A title given to architects. Usually capitalized or abbreviated as Arch./Ar. before the person's name.
To design, plan, or orchestrate.
definitions.example: He architected the military coup against the government.

definitions.definition: To invite; bid; ask.
An administrative division of the county of Kent, in England, from the Anglo-Saxon period until it fell entirely out of use in the early twentieth century.
A machine tool used to shape a piece of material, or workpiece, by rotating the workpiece against a cutting tool.
definitions.example: He shaped the bedpost by turning it on a lathe.

definitions.definition: The movable swing frame of a loom, carrying the reed for separating the warp threads and beating up the weft; a lay, or batten.
A granary; a barn.
To shape with a lathe.
To produce a three-dimensional model by rotating a set of points around a fixed axis.
Facilities for urination and defecation: a toilet; a lavatory.
The fact of being easy, or easily done; absence of difficulty, simplicity.
Dexterity of speech or action; skill, talent.
definitions.example: The facility she shows in playing the violin is unrivalled.

definitions.definition: The physical means or contrivances to make something (especially a public service) possible; the required equipment, infrastructure, location etc.
definitions.example: Transport facilities in Bangkok are not sufficient to prevent frequent traffic collapses during rush hour.

definitions.definition: An institution specially designed for a specific purpose, such as incarceration, military use, or scientific experimentation.
(in the plural) A toilet.
A condition of mental weakness less than idiocy, but enough to make a person easily persuaded to do something against their better interest.
Affability.
The result or outcome of a cause.

definitions.definition: Impression left on the mind; sensation produced.
Execution; performance; realization; operation.
An illusion produced by technical means (as in "special effect")
definitions.example: The effect of flying was most convincing.

definitions.definition: (sound engineering) An alteration, or device for producing an alteration, in sound after it has been produced by an instrument.
definitions.example: I just bought a couple of great effects.

definitions.definition: (etc.) A scientific phenomenon, usually named after its discoverer.
definitions.example: Doppler effect

definitions.definition: (usually in the plural) Belongings, usually as personal effects.
Consequence intended; purpose; meaning; general intent; with to.
Reality; actual meaning; fact, as distinguished from mere appearance.
Manifestation; expression; sign.
To make or bring about; to implement.
definitions.example: The best way to effect change is to work with existing stakeholders.

definitions.definition: To send out or give off
Ability to produce a desired effect under ideal testing conditions.
Degree of ability to produce a desired effect.
Something that is produced, consumed, used, or done every day.
To drive an automobile frequently, on a daily basis, for regular and mundane tasks.
That occurs every day, or at least every working day
Diurnal, by daylight, as opposed to nightly
To have enough courage (to do something).

definitions.definition: To defy or challenge (someone to do something)
definitions.example: I dare you (to) kiss that girl.

definitions.definition: To have enough courage to meet or do something, go somewhere, etc.; to face up to
definitions.example: Will you dare death to reach your goal?

definitions.definition: To terrify; to daunt.
To catch (larks) by producing terror through the use of mirrors, scarlet cloth, a hawk, etc., so that they lie still till a net is thrown over them.
To stare stupidly or vacantly; to gaze as though amazed or terrified.
To lie or crouch down in fear.
Boldness.
Adventurous, willing to take on or look for risks; overbold.
Courageous or showing bravery; doughty.
Racy; sexually provocative.
A small short-legged smooth-coated scenthound, often tricolored and sometimes used for hunting hares. Its friendly disposition makes it suitable as a family pet.
A person who snoops on others; a detective.
A bailiff.
A small kind of shark.
To hunt with beagles.
To search.
The art and technology of producing images on photosensitive surfaces, and its digital counterpart.
The occupation of taking (and often printing) photographs.
To be under an obligation to give something back to someone or to perform some action for someone.
To have debt; to be in debt.
Practiced; self-conscious; careful.
definitions.example: She gave a studied reply.

definitions.definition: Qualified by, or versed in, study; learned.
(usually academic) To review materials already learned in order to make sure one does not forget them, usually in preparation for an examination.
definitions.example: I need to study my biology notes.

definitions.definition: (academic) To take a course or courses on a subject.
definitions.example: I study medicine at the university.

definitions.definition: To acquire knowledge on a subject with the intention of applying it in practice.
definitions.example: Biologists study living things.

definitions.definition: To look at minutely.
definitions.example: He studied the map in preparation for the hike.

definitions.definition: To fix the mind closely upon a subject; to dwell upon anything in thought; to muse; to ponder.
To endeavor diligently; to be zealous.
To dull the senses or capacity to think thereby reducing responsiveness; to dazzle or stun.
The quality of being amusing, comical, funny.
definitions.example: She has a great sense of humour, and I always laugh a lot whenever we get together.

definitions.definition: A mood, especially a bad mood; a temporary state of mind or disposition brought upon by an event; an abrupt illogical inclination or whim.
definitions.example: He was in a particularly vile humour that afternoon.

definitions.definition: Any of the fluids in an animal body, especially the four "cardinal humours" of blood, yellow bile, black bile and phlegm that were believed to control the health and mood of the human body.
Either of the two regions of liquid within the eyeball, the aqueous humour and vitreous humour.
Moist vapour, moisture.
To pacify by indulging.
definitions.example: I know you don't believe my story, but humour me for a minute and imagine it to be true.

definitions.definition: An instance of hijacking; the illegal seizure of a vehicle; a hijacking.
An instance of a seizure and redirection of a process.
An amendment which deletes the contents of a bill and inserts entirely new provisions.
Preflop, the position two before the dealer.
To forcibly stop and seize control of some vehicle in order to rob it or to reach a destination (especially an airplane, truck or a boat).
To seize control of some process or resource to achieve a purpose other than its originally intended one.
To seize control of a networked computer by means of infecting it with a worm or other malware, thereby turning it into a zombie.
To change software settings without a user's knowledge so as to force that user to visit a certain web site (to hijack a browser).
To introduce an amendment deleting the contents of a bill and inserting entirely new provisions.
An exhibition of works from an extended period of an artist's activity.
Of, relating to, or contemplating the past.
Looking backwards.
Affecting or influencing past things; retroactive.
A course of action to be followed regularly; a standard procedure.
A set of normal procedures, often performed mechanically.
definitions.example: Connie was completely robotic and emotionless by age 12; her entire life had become one big routine.

definitions.definition: A set piece of an entertainer's act.
definitions.example: stand-up comedy routine

definitions.definition: A set of instructions designed to perform a specific task; a subroutine.
Unfortunate, marked by misfortune.
Inauspicious.
Having ill luck.
Bringing ill luck.
The natural world; that which consists of all things unaffected by or predating human technology, production, and design. (Compare ecosystem.)
definitions.example: Nature never lies (i.e. tells untruths).

definitions.definition: The innate characteristics of a thing. What something will tend by its own constitution, to be or do. Distinct from what might be expected or intended.
The summary of everything that has to do with biological, chemical and physical states and events in the physical universe.
Conformity to that which is natural, as distinguished from that which is artificial, or forced, or remote from actual experience.
Kind, sort; character; quality.
Physical constitution or existence; the vital powers; the natural life.
Natural affection or reverence.
To endow with natural qualities.
A person who performs, plays a part in a theatrical play or film.
One who acts; a doer.
One who takes part in a situation.
An advocate or proctor in civil courts or causes.
One who institutes a suit; plaintiff or complainant.
(policy debate) One who enacts a certain policy action.
The entity that performs a role (in use case analysis).
(grammar) The most agent-like argument of a clause, e.g. 'the torpedo' in "The torpedo sank the boat" and "The torpedo fired".
Having an end or limit; (of a quantity) constrained by bounds; (of a set) whose number of elements is a natural number.
(grammar, as opposed to infinite or nonfinite) limited by person or number.
definitions.example: The "goes" in "he goes" is a finite form of a verb

definitions.definition: A brush, used with toothpaste, for cleaning the teeth.
To clean or scrub with a toothbrush.
One thousand (103) watts.
A shift (scheduled work period) that takes place early in the day.
At a time in advance of the usual or expected event.
definitions.example: at eleven, we went for an early lunch;  she began reading at an early age;  his mother suffered an early death

definitions.definition: Arriving a time before expected; sooner than on time.
definitions.example: The early guests sipped their punch and avoided each other's eyes.

definitions.definition: Near the start or beginning.
definitions.example: Early results showed their winning 245 out of 300 seats in parliament. The main opponent locked up only 31 seats.

definitions.definition: Having begun to occur; in its early stages.
definitions.example: early cancer

definitions.definition: Of a star or class of stars, hotter than the sun.
At a time before expected; sooner than usual.
definitions.example: We finished the project an hour sooner than scheduled, so we left early.

definitions.definition: Soon; in good time; seasonably.
The act or process of dividing anything.
Each of the separate parts of something resulting from division.
The process of dividing a number by another.
A calculation that involves this process.
definitions.example: I've got ten divisions to do for my homework.

definitions.definition: A formation, usually made up of two or three brigades.
A usually high-level section of a large company or conglomerate.
A rank below kingdom and above class, particularly used of plants or fungi, also (particularly of animals) called a phylum; a taxon at that rank.
definitions.example: Magnolias belong to the division Magnoliophyta.

definitions.definition: A disagreement; a difference of viewpoint between two sides of an argument.
A method by which a legislature is separated into groups in order to take a better estimate of vote than a voice vote.
definitions.example: The House of Commons has voted to approve the third reading of the bill without a division. The bill will now progress to the House of Lords.

definitions.definition: A florid instrumental variation of a melody in the 17th and 18th centuries, originally conceived as the dividing of each of a succession of long notes into several short ones.
A set of pipes in a pipe organ which are independently controlled and supplied.
A concept whereby a common group of debtors are only responsible for their proportionate sum of the total debt.
Any of the four major parts of a COBOL program source code
(Eton College) A lesson; a class.
To cook in a small amount of liquid, in a covered pan, somewhere between steaming and boiling.
To join two metal pieces, without melting them, using heat and diffusion of a jointing alloy of capillary thickness.
To burn or temper in fire.
A large, swollen, or protruding abdomen; a paunch.
A potbelly stove.
A Vietnamese Pot-bellied pig.
To store in a warehouse or similar.
To confine (a person) to an institution for a long period.
To acquire and then shelve, simply to prevent competitors from acquiring it.
definitions.example: the warehousing of syndicated TV shows

definitions.definition: The act of storing goods in a warehouse.
The manner in which something is swung.
definitions.example: Door swing indicates direction the door opens.

definitions.definition: The sweep or compass of a swinging body.
A line, cord, or other thing suspended and hanging loose, upon which anything may swing.
A hanging seat in a children's playground, for acrobats in a circus, or on a porch for relaxing.
A dance style.
The genre of music associated with this dance style.
The amount of change towards or away from something.
Sideways movement of the ball as it flies through the air.
Capacity of a turning lathe, as determined by the diameter of the largest object that can be turned in it.
In a musical theater production, a performer who understudies several roles.
A basic dance step in which a pair link hands and turn round together in a circle.
Free course; unrestrained liberty.
Influence or power of anything put in motion.
A type of hook with the arm more extended.
To rotate about an off-centre fixed point.
definitions.example: The plant swung in the breeze.

definitions.definition: To dance.
To ride on a swing.
definitions.example: The children laughed as they swung.

definitions.definition: To participate in the swinging lifestyle; to participate in wife-swapping.
To hang from the gallows.
(of a ball) to move sideways in its trajectory.
To fluctuate or change.
definitions.example: It wasn't long before the crowd's mood swung towards restless irritability.

definitions.definition: To move (an object) backward and forward; to wave.
definitions.example: He swung his sword as hard as he could.

definitions.definition: To change (a numerical result); especially to change the outcome of an election.
To make (something) work; especially to afford (something) financially.
definitions.example: If it’s not too expensive, I think we can swing it.

definitions.definition: To play notes that are in pairs by making the first of the pair slightly longer than written (augmentation) and the second shorter, resulting in a bouncy, uneven rhythm.
(of a bowler) to make the ball move sideways in its trajectory.
To move one's arm in a punching motion.
In dancing, to turn around in a small circle with one's partner, holding hands or arms.
definitions.example: "to swing one's partner", or simply "to swing"

definitions.definition: To admit or turn something for the purpose of shaping it; said of a lathe.
definitions.example: The lathe can swing a pulley of 12 inches diameter.

definitions.definition: To put (a door, gate, etc.) on hinges so that it can swing or turn.
To turn round by action of wind or tide when at anchor.
definitions.example: A ship swings with the tide.

definitions.definition: An intense distressing emotion of fear or repugnance.
Something horrible; that which excites horror.
definitions.example: I saw many horrors during the war.

definitions.definition: Intense dislike or aversion; an abhorrence.
A genre of fiction designed to evoke a feeling of fear and suspense.
An individual work in this genre.
A nasty or ill-behaved person; a rascal or terror.
definitions.example: The neighbour's kids are a pack of little horrors!

definitions.definition: An intense anxiety or a nervous depression; often the horrors.
(plural) Delirium tremens.
A rocket engine.
A non-guided missile propelled by a rocket engine.
A vehicle propelled by a rocket engine.
A rocket propelled firework, a skyrocket
An ace (the playing card).
An angry communication (such as a letter or telegram) to a subordinate.
A blunt lance head used in jousting.
Something that shoots high in the air.
A stupid or crazy person.
To accelerate swiftly and powerfully
To fly vertically
To rise or soar rapidly
To carry something in a rocket
To attack something with rockets
Whole, not divided into parts.
definitions.example: After the vase had fallen down the flight of stairs we were amazed to find it still unbroken.

definitions.definition: Of a horse, not tamed.
definitions.example: There is something majestic about the spirit of an unbroken mustang as it runs wild across the prairie.

definitions.definition: Continuous, without interruption.
definitions.example: The team's unbroken winning streak was a record.

definitions.definition: A lighter-than-air aircraft that can be propelled forward through the air as well as steered.
definitions.example: Airships are posited to be cheaper to operate over time than fixed-wing aircraft, but as there are no large fleets, this is hard to prove in practice.

definitions.definition: Any aircraft.
definitions.example: On weekends, I liked to spend my time at the airport watching the various airships take off and land.

definitions.definition: The highest ranking officials of the RAF, viewed as arrogant and distant.
To transport goods by aircraft.
Word for word; not figuratively; not as an idiom or metaphor
definitions.example: When I saw on the news that there would be no school tomorrow because of the snowstorm, I literally jumped for joy, and hit my head on the ceiling fan.

definitions.definition: (degree, contranym) Used non-literally as an intensifier for figurative statements: virtually, so to speak (often considered incorrect; see usage notes)
definitions.example: He was so surprised, he literally jumped twenty feet in the air.

definitions.definition: Used to intensify or dramatise non-figurative statements; tending towards a meaningless filler word in repeated use.
definitions.example: I had no idea, so I was literally guessing.

definitions.definition: Used as a generic downtoner: just, merely.
definitions.example: You literally put it in the microwave for five minutes and it's done.

definitions.definition: An unoccupied position or job.
An available room in a hotel; guest house, etc.
Empty space.
Lack of intelligence or understanding.
A defect in a crystal caused by the absence of an atom in a lattice
A glycopeptide antibiotic, produced by the actinomycete Amycolaptosis orientalis, used against Gram-positive bacteria, especially staphylococci and enterococci.
A government in which a single ruler (a tyrant) has absolute power; this system of government.
The office or jurisdiction of an absolute ruler.
Absolute power, or its use.
A system of government in which power is exercised on behalf of the ruler or ruling class, without regard to the wishes of the governed.
Extreme severity or rigour.
A piece of equipment that may be strapped to a person’s back and generate vertical thrust by a downward discharge of jet fluid, thereby rendering the person capable of flight.
To travel with a jet pack.
definitions.example: the first person to jetpack around the world

definitions.definition: Dawn.
Tune; sequence of notes that makes up a musical phrase
Something that is possibly true.
An answer that shows neither agreement nor disagreement.
definitions.example: The results of the poll were inconclusive. We got two yeses, three nos, and four maybes.

definitions.definition: A future event that may or may not happen.
definitions.example: About your raise: it's a big maybe.

definitions.definition: Possible; uncertain.
Modifies a verb, indicating a lack of certainty: it may be that...
(as a pro-sentence) Perhaps that is true (expressing no commitment to a decision or a neutral viewpoint to a statement).
In a miraculous manner.
A flammable liquid consisting of a mixture of refined petroleum hydrocarbons, mainly used as a motor fuel; petrol.
Any specific kind of gasoline.
definitions.example: The refinery produces a wide range of gasolines.

definitions.definition: Made from or using gasoline.
One of a set of choices that can be made.
The freedom or right to choose.

A contract giving the holder the right to buy or sell an asset at a set strike price; can apply to financial market transactions, or to ordinary transactions for tangible assets such as a residence or automobile.
To purchase an option on something.
definitions.example: The new novel was optioned by the film studio, but they'll probably never decide to make a movie from it.

definitions.definition: To configure, by setting an option.
Recently found; newly discovered.
A chemical element (symbol Nb) with an atomic number of 41: a light grey, crystalline, ductile transition metal used in superconducting materials.
A single atom of this element.
In need; poor.
definitions.example: Needy people want to give too, but have few material goods to offer.

definitions.definition: Desiring constant affirmation; lacking self-confidence.
definitions.example: It's emotionally exhausting to be around her because she's so needy.

definitions.definition: Needful; necessary.
A group of people other than a jury.
Not a jury, or not involving a jury.
Another time; once more.
Over and above a factor of one.
Used metalinguistically, with the repetition being in the discussion, or in the linguistic or pragmatic context of the discussion, rather than in the subject of discussion.
definitions.example: Great, thanks again!

definitions.definition: Back in the reverse direction, or to an original starting point.
definitions.example: Bring us word again.

definitions.definition: Back (to a former place or state).
definitions.example: The South will rise again.

definitions.definition: In return, as a reciprocal action; back.
In any other place.
On the other hand.
Moreover; besides; further.
(obsolete or dialectal) Against.
Brought from far away.
Not likely; difficult to believe.
definitions.example: He is full of far-fetched ideas to make money.

definitions.definition: A train that calls at only some stations it passes between its origin and destination, typically just the principal stations
Firmly or securely fixed in place; stable.
definitions.example: That rope is dangerously loose. Make it fast!

definitions.definition: Firm against attack; fortified by nature or art; impregnable; strong.
(of people) Steadfast, with unwavering feeling. (Now mostly in set phrases like fast friend(s).)
Moving with great speed, or capable of doing so; swift, rapid.
definitions.example: I am going to buy a fast car.

definitions.definition: Causing unusual rapidity of play or action.
definitions.example: a fast billiard table

definitions.definition: (of a piece of hardware) Able to transfer data in a short period of time.
Deep or sound (of sleep); fast asleep (of people).
(of dyes or colours) Not running or fading when subjected to detrimental conditions such as wetness or intense light; permanent.
definitions.example: All the washing has come out pink. That red tee-shirt was not fast.

definitions.definition: Tenacious; retentive.
Having an extravagant lifestyle or immoral habits.
definitions.example: a fast woman

definitions.definition: Ahead of the correct time or schedule.
definitions.example: There must be something wrong with the hall clock. It is always fast.

definitions.definition: (of photographic film) More sensitive to light than average.
In a firm or secure manner, securely; in such a way as not to be moved; safe, sound .
definitions.example: Hold this rope as fast as you can.

definitions.definition: (of sleeping) Deeply or soundly .
definitions.example: He is fast asleep.

definitions.definition: Immediately following in place or time; close, very near .
definitions.example: Fast by the sturdy batsman the ball unheeded sped. / That ain't my style, said Casey. Strike one, the umpire said.

definitions.definition: Quickly, with great speed; within a short time .
definitions.example: Do it as fast as you can.

definitions.definition: Ahead of the correct time or schedule.
definitions.example: I think my watch is running fast.

definitions.definition: Short for "stand fast", a warning not to pass between the arrow and the target
Something being indicated that is there; one of those.
(degree) To a given extent or degree.
definitions.example: "The ribbon was that thin." "I disagree, I say it was not that thin, it was thicker... or maybe thinner..."

definitions.definition: (degree) To a great extent or degree; very, particularly (in negative constructions).
definitions.example: I did the run last year, and it wasn't that difficult.

definitions.definition: To such an extent; so. (in positive constructions).
definitions.example: Ooh, I was that happy I nearly kissed her.

definitions.definition: (demonstrative) The thing, person, idea, quality, event, action or time indicated or understood from context, especially if more remote geographically, temporally or mentally than one designated as "this", or if expressing distinction.
definitions.example: He went home, and after that I never saw him again.

definitions.definition: The known (thing); used to refer to something just said.
definitions.example: They're getting divorced. What do you think about that?

definitions.definition: (demonstrative) The aforementioned quality or proposition; used to emphatically affirm or deny a previous statement or question.
definitions.example: The water is so cold! — That it is.

definitions.definition: (relative) (plural that) Which, who; representing a subject, direct object, indirect object, or object of a preposition.
definitions.example: The CPR course that she took really came in handy.

definitions.definition: Used in place of relative adverbs such as where or when; often omitted.
definitions.example: the last time that [= when] I went to Europe

definitions.definition: Introducing a clause which is the subject or object of a verb (such as one involving reported speech), or which is a complement to a previous statement.
definitions.example: He told me that the book is a good read.

definitions.definition: Introducing a subordinate clause expressing a reason or cause: because, in that.
definitions.example: Be glad that you have enough to eat.

definitions.definition: Introducing a subordinate clause that expresses an aim, purpose or goal ("final"), and usually contains the auxiliaries may, might or should: so, so that.
Introducing — especially, but not exclusively, with an antecedent like so or such — a subordinate clause expressing a result, consequence or effect.
definitions.example: The noise was so loud that she woke up.

definitions.definition: Introducing a premise or supposition for consideration: seeing as; inasmuch as; given that; as would appear from the fact that.
Introducing a subordinate clause modifying an adverb.
definitions.example: How often did she visit him? — Twice that I saw.

definitions.definition: Introducing an exclamation expressing a desire or wish.
definitions.example: Oh that spring would come!

definitions.definition: Introducing an exclamation expressing a strong emotion such as sadness or surprise.

In the International System of Units, the base unit of mass; conceived of as the mass of one litre of water, but now defined by taking the fixed numerical value of the Planck constant h to be 6.626 070 15 × 10-34 when expressed in units of kg⋅m2⋅s−1. Symbol: kg
The unit of weight such that a one-kilogram mass is also a one-kilogram weight.
One who jests, jokes or teases.
A person in colourful garb and fool's cap who amused a medieval and early modern royal or noble court.
Any of various nymphalid butterflies of the Southeast Asian genus Symbrenthia.
An authoritative principle, belief or statement of opinion, especially one considered to be absolutely true and indisputable, regardless of evidence or without evidence to support it.
A doctrine (or set of doctrines) relating to matters such as morality and faith, set forth authoritatively by a religious organization or leader.
definitions.example: In the Catholic Church, new dogmas can only be declared by the pope after the extremely rare procedure ex cathedra to make them part of the official faith.

definitions.definition: In a brave manner.
In the place of something (usually mentioned earlier); as a substitute or alternative.
definitions.example: I was going to go shopping, but I went dancing instead.

definitions.definition: Any of various devices that are used to inject something.
An object that realizes a dependency injection.
An event that appears inexplicable by the laws of nature and so is held to be supernatural in origin.
definitions.example: An example of a miracle associated with Muhammad is the splitting of the moon.

definitions.definition: A fortunate outcome that prevails despite overwhelming odds against it.
An awesome and exceptional example of something
To affect by a miracle; to work a miracle upon.
A small door or gate, especially one beside a larger one.
A small window or other opening, sometimes fitted with a grating.
A service window, as in a bank or train station, where a customer conducts transactions with a teller; a ticket barrier at a rail station, box office at a cinema, etc.
One of the two wooden structures at each end of the pitch, consisting of three vertical stumps and two bails; the target for the bowler, defended by the batsman.
A dismissal; the act of a batsman getting out.
The period during which two batsmen bat together.
The pitch.
The area around the stumps where the batsmen stand.
Any of the small arches through which the balls are driven.
A temporary metal attachment that one attaches one's lift-ticket to.
A shelter made from tree boughs, used by lumbermen.
The space between the pillars, in post-and-stall working.
An angle bracket when used in HTML.
Dissatisfied and irritable.
definitions.example: The old man was grumpy because he couldn’t find his glasses.

definitions.definition: Someone possessing extraordinary intelligence or skill; especially somebody who has demonstrated this by a creative or original work in science, music, art etc.
definitions.example: I think that would qualify as not smart, but genius... and a very stable genius at that!

definitions.definition: Extraordinary mental capacity.
Inspiration, a mental leap, an extraordinary creative process.
definitions.example: a work of genius.

definitions.definition: The tutelary deity or spirit of a place or person.
Ingenious, brilliant, very clever, or original.
definitions.example: What a genius idea!

definitions.definition: A man of gentle but not noble birth, particularly a man of means (originally ownership of property) who does not work for a living but has no official status in a peerage; an armiferous man ranking below a knight.
definitions.example: Being a gentleman, Robert was entitled to shove other commoners into the gongpit but he still had to jump out of the way of the knights to avoid the same fate himself.

definitions.definition: Any well-bred, well-mannered, or charming man.
An effeminate or oversophisticated man.
definitions.example: Well, la-di-da, aren't you just a proper gentleman?

definitions.definition: (polite term of address) Any man.
definitions.example: Please escort this gentleman to the gentlemen's room.

definitions.definition: (usually historical, sometimes derogatory) An amateur or dabbler in any field, particularly those of independent means.
An amateur player, particularly one whose wealth permits him to forego payment.
Possessive case of gentlemen: belonging to some or all gentlemen.
A lavatory intended for use by men, often including urinals in addition to toilets.
Other rooms intended for use by men, as waiting rooms, dressing rooms, locker rooms, etc.
A shrine dedicated to some prophetic deity.
A person such as a priest through whom the deity is supposed to respond with prophecy or advice.
A prophetic response, often enigmatic or allegorical, so given.
A person considered to be a source of wisdom.
definitions.example: a literary oracle

definitions.definition: A wise sentence or decision of great authority.
One who communicates a divine command; an angel; a prophet.
A theoretical entity capable of answering some collection of questions.
(Jewish antiquity) The sanctuary, or most holy place in the temple; also, the temple itself.
To utter oracles or prophecies.
Sucrose in the form of small crystals, obtained from sugar cane or sugar beet and used to sweeten food and drink.
A specific variety of sugar.
Any of various small carbohydrates that are used by organisms to store energy.
A small serving of this substance (typically about one teaspoon), used to sweeten a drink.
definitions.example: He usually has his coffee white with one sugar.

definitions.definition: A term of endearment.
definitions.example: I'll be with you in a moment, sugar.

definitions.definition: A kiss.
Effeminacy in a male, often implying homosexuality.
definitions.example: I think John has a little bit of sugar in him.

definitions.definition: Diabetes.
Anything resembling sugar in taste or appearance, especially in chemistry.
definitions.example: Sugar of lead (lead acetate) is a poisonous white crystalline substance with a sweet taste.

definitions.definition: Compliment or flattery used to disguise or render acceptable something obnoxious; honeyed or soothing words.
Heroin.
Money.
Syntactic sugar.
To add sugar to; to sweeten with sugar.
definitions.example: John heavily sugars his coffee.

definitions.definition: To make (something unpleasant) seem less so.
definitions.example: She has a gift for sugaring what would otherwise be harsh words.

definitions.definition: In making maple sugar, to complete the process of boiling down the syrup till it is thick enough to crystallize; to approach or reach the state of granulation; with the preposition off.
To apply sugar to trees or plants in order to catch moths.
To rewrite (source code) using syntactic sugar.
To compliment (a person).
Used in place of shit!
definitions.example: Oh, sugar!

definitions.definition: In a way or manner that is real, not unreal.
(modal) Actually; in fact; in reality.
definitions.example: "He really is a true friend." / "Really? What makes you so sure?"

definitions.definition: (as an intensifier) Very (modifying an adjective); very much (modifying a verb).
definitions.example: But ma, I really, really want to go to the show!

definitions.definition: Indicating surprise at, or requesting confirmation of, some new information; to express skepticism.
definitions.example: A: He won the Nobel Prize yesterday.

definitions.definition: (sarcastic, typically exaggerated question.) Indicating that what was just said was obvious and unnecessary; contrived incredulity
definitions.example: A: I've just been reading Shakespeare - he's one of the best authors like, ever!

definitions.definition: Indicating affirmation, agreement.
definitions.example: A: That girl talks about herself way too much.

definitions.definition: Indicating displeasure at another person's behaviour or statement.
definitions.example: Well, really! How rude.

definitions.definition: A place where nursing or the raising of children is carried on.
A place where anything is fostered and growth promoted.
Something which educates and nurtures.
definitions.example: Commerce is the nursery of seamen.

definitions.definition: Short for nursery cannon.
Someone or something that is nursed; a nursling.
The act of nursing or rearing.
A spasmodic, nervous system disease brought on by the tetanus bacteria. It causes muscles to seize up and may cause death by suffocation.
The power unit of a train that pulls the coaches or wagons.
A traction engine
A cheer characterized by a slow beginning and a progressive increase in speed
A country which drives the world economy by having a high level of imports. (i.e. The United States).
Of or relating to locomotion
Of or relating to the power unit of a train which does not carry passengers or freight itself
One who makes or enacts laws.
A video, image or text spread by "word of mouth" on the internet or by e-mail for humorous, political or marketing purposes.
Of or relating to a biological virus.
definitions.example: viral DNA

definitions.definition: Caused by a virus.
definitions.example: viral infection

definitions.definition: Of the nature of an informatic virus; able to spread copies of itself to other computers.
Spread by word of mouth, with minimal intervention in order to create buzz and interest.
(of an image, video, piece of information, etc.) circulated rapidly and widely from one Internet user to another.
An itinerant tinsmith and mender of household utensils made of metal.
A member of the Irish Traveller community. A gypsy.
(usually with "little") A mischievous person, especially a playful, impish youngster.
Someone who repairs, or attempts repair, on anything mechanical, or who invents such devices; one who tinkers; a tinkerer.
The act of repair or invention.
A hand mortar.
Any of various fish: the chub mackerel, the silverside, the skate, or a young mackerel about two years old.
A bird, the razor-billed auk.
To fiddle with something in an attempt to fix, mend or improve it, especially in an experimental or unskilled manner.
To work as a tinker.
To tinker with; to tweak or attempt to fix.
